headline: AI coding hits warp speed—Cursor at $50B talks, Replit Agent 4 ships parallel builds, Claude Code Revenue doubles to $2.5B

stories.title: Replit Agent 4 launches with parallel agents and infinite design canvas
stories.summary: <cite index="49-3,49-4">Replit officially launched Agent 4 on March 11, 2026, representing a major leap in how developers can build and ship software with built-in philosophy that devs should focus on creating, not coordinating</cite>. <cite index="50-5,50-6">Parallel agent execution lets independent tasks run simultaneously—authentication, databases, backend, and frontend can all be in progress at once</cite>.
stories.takeaway: vibe coding just went parallel—ship full apps 10x faster by running multiple agent tasks at once instead of waiting for one to finish

stories.title: Cursor talks $50B valuation, drops ACP for JetBrains IDEs Mar 4
stories.summary: <cite index="21-1,21-2">Cursor is in talks with investors for a funding round that would value it at about $50 billion, nearly double the valuation from last fall</cite>. <cite index="20-1,20-3,20-4">Cursor became available in JetBrains IDEs through Agent Client Protocol, letting developers use frontier models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Cursor for agent-driven development</cite>.
stories.takeaway: Cursor's enterprise dominance keeps growing—now Java/multilang devs can use it natively in JetBrains without leaving their favorite IDE

stories.title: Claude Code hits $2.5B ARR, Anthropic doubles quotas Mar 13-27
stories.summary: <cite index="41-3,41-4,41-20">Claude Code annualized revenue topped $1 billion by end of 2025, then more than doubled to $2.5 billion by February, putting Anthropic on track to surpass OpenAI's revenue by end of 2026</cite>. <cite index="47-1,47-2">From March 13-27, Anthropic doubles usage quotas across all plans outside 8 AM-2 PM ET, no action required</cite>.
stories.takeaway: Claude Code's dominance is real—$2.5B ARR shows enterprise adoption crushing, plus they're lowkey easing quota friction to lock in users

stories.title: GitHub Copilot CLI hits 1.0, adds /pr command and autopilot timing Mar 2
stories.summary: <cite index="28-6,28-7">GitHub announced Copilot CLI 1.0 after general availability, incrementing the major version to commemorate reaching GA</cite>. <cite index="33-5,33-6">New /pr command helps create and view PRs, automatically fix CI failures, and Copilot CLI brings coding agent power directly to the terminal</cite>.
stories.takeaway: Copilot CLI's GA sprint complete—now it's terminal-first agentic dev with native PR flows, still competing hard with Cursor's IDE dominance

watchNext.title: Cursor's $50B Talks Signal Enterprise War—Can Copilot/Claude Keep Up?
watchNext.why: Cursor's valuation climb shows enterprise's chosen their agentic IDE, but Copilot's multi-IDE strategy + Claude Code's $2.5B ARR suggest this market's big enough for winners across different workflows

watchNext.title: Simon Willison on AI-Assisted Coding's Divide: Outcome-Driven vs Craft-Lovers
watchNext.why: fr, Simon's take on how coding agents are exposing a real split in dev motivation—some want outcomes fast, others care about process. understanding this shapes how tools evolve next
